title: TanStack Start Full Course 2026 | Build and Deploy a Full Stack Application
summary: A full-course tutorial on TanStack Start, a framework addressing hydration errors and RSC directive complexity. The course covers routing, data fetching, server functions, API routes, SEO best practices, and includes a complete full-stack marketplace application (Skild) built from scratch to deployment.
writeup: This video is a full-course introduction to TanStack Start, a full-stack React framework built on TanStack Router and Vite, framed around building a marketplace for reusable AI agent skills. The instructor makes the architectural case against Next.js by pinpointing three recurring pain points: hydration errors, scattered use-client and use-server directives, and vendor lock-in. The through-line is a request-to-paint mental model that traces how a URL request flows through route matching, parallel loaders, server-side rendering, and hydration into a client-side SPA, so the viewer can reason precisely about the bugs that arise when this pipeline breaks. The stack combines TanStack Start with React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind, Clerk, and Firebase.
key_points: TanStack Start eliminates the use-client and use-server directive problem by confining server work to the route loader level rather than scattering it inside JSX, so React components remain plain React components with no boundary annotations.

End-to-end type safety flows automatically from database query through TanStack Router to the React component: changing a database query surfaces a type error in the editor immediately, which Next.js does not provide by default.
Server functions replace TRPC and a separate API layer for mutations; they validate input automatically and maintain type safety from front to back without additional installation.
Because TanStack Start is built on Vite, it deploys to any JavaScript runtime including Vercel, Cloudflare, Netlify, and a plain Node server, with no platform lock-in.

The SSR request lifecycle runs in strictly this order: URL-to-route match, parallel loader execution (data fetching before React renders), server-side render to an HTML string the browser paints immediately, then hydration so subsequent navigation runs as a client-side SPA without full reloads.

Major infrastructure partners including Cloudflare, Netlify, Clerk, and Neon are actively investing in TanStack Start as a first-class deployment target, indicating early industry-level adoption momentum.

chapters.title: SSR, Streaming, and Built-In Server Functions
